original_abstract: Hashing has become a cornerstone of multimedia similarity search due to its minimal storage requirements and exceptional query efficiency. Recently, there has been a growing emphasis on multimodal hashing to handle complex data, such as image-tag pairs. many practical scenarios provide supervised semantic labels, leading to the development of supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) techniques that leverage this information to enhance retrieval precision. However, many current SMH approaches suffer from high training time complexity, severely limiting their performance on large-scale datasets. To address this challenge, we introduce Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM), a novel SMH framework designed to seamlessly integrate semantic labels into the hash learning process. By optimizing for large-scale data modeling, SCM maintains high efficiency without sacrificing precision. Experimental evaluations on two real-world datasets demonstrate that SCM significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in both search accuracy and computational scalability.
humanized_abstract: Hashing has been widely used in multimedia similarity search applications because of the storage efficiency and fast search speed. In recent years, multimodal hashing for complex data like image-tag pairs has attracted increasing attention. A large number of practical applications provide supervised semantic labels of media samples. Therefore, improving the search accuracy is crucial, which motivates the development of supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) methods. However, many existing SMH methods are inefficient for large-scale search applications, because their training time complexity is still high. In this paper, we propose a lightweight Supervised Multimodal Hashing (SMH) framework named Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM), which not only explores the relationships between heterogeneous data samples and their corresponding semantic labels but also realizes a trade-off between the modeling capability for large-scale data and the training speed. The experimental results on the widely used large-scale image search benchmarks (Scene15 and MIR-Flickr-500) confirm the superiority of SCM in terms of search accuracy and speed compared with the state-of-the-art hashing baselines.
